name: Brian Lynch
abstract: Brian Lynch (September 12, 1956) is an American jazz trumpeter. He has been a member of Eddie Palmieri's Afro-Caribbean Jazz group and has led the Latin Side of Miles project with trombonist Conrad Herwig.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Tito Puente's Golden Latin Jazz All-Stars/ Eddie Palmieri and His Afro-Cuban Jazz Octet

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: I had Harry Leahey first on guitar, then Tom Harrell, I added him on trumpet, and then Hal Crook on trombone, then Brian Lynch. That’s been the front line.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: Anything with the quintet I’m really proud of all the quintet stuff, whether it’s with Hal Crook or Tom Harrell or Brian Lynch.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: we were going to have the quintet with Brian Lynch, Phil, and Bill Charlap, Steve Gilmore and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Charles McPherson
about_quotes.evidence: You know Brian Lynch, really wonderful trumpet player, a good friend.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles McPherson interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 9, 2024 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: but we had there Brian Lynch, Conrad Herwig, and Donald Harrison...
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
